title: Édition manifeste
sources: App.tsx: Header and core state management [cite: 6]
database.sql: PostgreSQL schema for memos and users [cite: 8]
resume: The quest "Édition manifeste" (A3) represents the active "Chantier" or work-in-progress phase of the community’s foundational vision[cite: 5]. Following the initial draft created in A2, this stage focuses on the meticulous refinement and collaborative editing of manifesto to ensure it perfectly aligns with the mission of "Saving human communities and long-term memory"[cite: 2, 5]. In the architectural framework of Forom, this editing process is not merely a textual update but a significant milestone in Phase 1: The Foundations[cite: 2]. During this phase, the community is tasked with filling the 10x10 FRM grid with high-quality content that will serve as the primary training data for the future AI assistant, ROM[cite: 2]. The software environment, powered by Vite 7 and React 19, provides a high-performance interface for members to interact with the grid, which uses a spatial 5x5 viewport to navigate categories like Vision[cite: 2, 6, 7]. Technically, any edits made are handled by the local database schema, specifically updating the 'description' and 'title' fields in the 'memos' table, which supports extensive text through the TEXT(max) data type[cite: 8]. The role of the "Creator" is vital here; these users are the innovators who push the mission forward by refining these core assets[cite: 2]. Economically, engaging in this chantier is incentivized by the Pixel (PX) economy[cite: 2]. In Phase 1, completing such a quest rewards the user with 2 PX, helping to redistribute purchasing power as the server grows toward its 1209-user capacity[cite: 2, 8]. This editing stage is crucial because it prepares the manifesto for its eventual transition into the V2 Archive during Phase 2: The Iteration, where the community will rewrite and improve upon these foundational 100 memos[cite: 2]. By hosting this data locally on hardware like an NVIDIA Jetson, the community ensures that their refined manifesto remains under their total ownership, protected from third-party cloud vulnerabilities[cite: 2]. This commitment to data sovereignty is what distinguishes Forom from standard social networks, turning a simple editing task into an act of preserving long-term community memory[cite: 2]. As the manifesto is edited, it becomes a cryptographic seed for the Phase 3 ritual, where the community treasury must hold twenty-five percent of total wealth to unlock ROM's full capabilities[cite: 2]. This step ensures the lore is canonized[cite: 5].
summary: This quest represents the collaborative refinement of the community manifesto at coordinate A3, preparing the vision for future AI ingestion.
